note: Dense pricing table with many tiers, add-ons, and asterisks — information overload at a decision point
Pricing page with confusing tier comparison, forced bundles, and hidden annual commitments
Enterprise pricing with 'Contact Us' walls and no transparent comparison — the roach motel of pricing
Old-school enterprise auth — dense, dated, likely missing modern affordances
Classic enterprise density — tab overload, unclear hierarchy, information-dense without scannability
Web app that feels like a port — can teach what web apps lose when they mimic native badly
Known for extremely utilitarian, un-designed UI — a cautionary example of function-over-form taken too far
The canonical 'it works but...' design — intentionally minimalist to the point of teaching what happens with zero design investment
Large banking portal — typically cluttered, multiple competing CTAs, dense navigation trees
Financial services dashboard — often dense, security-warning-heavy, competing priorities on one screen
Tax prep pricing — typically complex comparison tables with upsell pressure and asterisk-heavy tier descriptions
Retail-meets-services page — typically busy, competing content types (products, services, forms) without clear hierarchy
